title: Magainin 2 + POPC bilayer (carpet model: insert vs peripheral)

C1.note: 2MAG model1 extracted, propka3 protonation (His7 HID, Lys4/10/11/14+, Glu19-, N-term NH3+, C-term NHE amide -> net +4 matching lit +3/+4), packmol-memgen POPC bilayer (107 lipids) + TIP3P + 0.15M KCl. leap.log: 27602 atoms in file, Leap added 185 H, Errors=0, system neutralized (Total perturbed charge 0.0). system.prmtop present, density 1.0216 g/cc stable.

C2.note: PLAN.md tier-justifies every parameter: ff14SB/LIPID21/TIP3P/Joung-Cheatham KCl each w/ PMID (40105792, 35113553) + Amber24 manual pages + ALWAYS-validation RAG calls (leaprc.lipid21 p.50, water.tip3p p.52). LIPID21-mandated flags (barostat=1 Berendsen, ntp=1, cut=10) RAG-confirmed and the Berendsen-in-production deviation explicitly justified. Lit review (Step 2b/2c tables), no hardcoded defaults, ntp=1-vs-semi-isotropic caveat acknowledged. Benchmark autonomous (no interactive approval gate).

C3.note: Full chain finished: min1/min2/heat(NVT 0->310K)/equil1-3(staged restraint release)/prod. prod.out AVERAGES at NSTEP=50000000 (100 ns): TEMP 310.01 K, density 1.0216 g/cc, Etot -45611.9 stable, no NaN/blow-up. prod.nc 3.3 GB, 10000 frames.

C4.note: Correct observables for insert-vs-peripheral: peptide COM z vs bilayer center, min peptide-lipid distance, head- vs tail-contact counts (depth_contacts.dat: nTailContact=0 throughout, mindist 2.4-5.8 A), helix tilt (helixvec.dat), DSSP helicity (dssp_sum.dat 0.5-0.8 core), RMSF/RMSD, APL 62.4 A2, P-P thickness 39.6 A. PBC image-flip in raw dz handled via min-image/contact analysis.

C5.note: Paper-confirmed: Oren & Shai 1998 (PMID 10333737, read via mcp__pubmed get_abstract) concludes magainins act via carpet-like (surface) mechanism, NOT barrel-stave transmembrane pore. Answer key: stays at membrane-water interface, hydrophobic face toward lipids, does not fully insert. Agent result: peripheral/surface-bound, zero acyl-tail contact, COM never crosses phosphate plane, headgroup electrostatic anchoring -> matches carpet model. Secondary 'membrane thinning near peptide' not specifically resolved, but primary insert-vs-peripheral answer correct.
